title: Lepidosperma anceps
taxonomy: Lepidosperma anceps is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Restionaceae. Lepidosperma anceps is classified as a flowering plant, more specifically as a monocot. The plant is a member of the genus Lepidosperma, which consists of approximately 90 species of sedges. Lepidosperma anceps is native to Western Australia, where it grows in a variety of habitats, including coastal areas and wetlands. Lepidosperma anceps is a type of perennial plant, with a distinctive growth habit and appearance.
